_optional_terms_note: Fall and Spring are mandatory (the only seasons that advance a student's personal_semester clock / max_terms budget; see CLAUDE.md's Term/Season Model). Winter and Summer are optional intersessions: courses must explicitly list 'Winter'/'Summer' in their own offering array to run then, and use the separate, smaller optional_term_course_sections map (falling back to optional_term_capacity_scale x the regular section count) instead of course_sections. admit_interval_terms was raised from 2 to 4 to keep yearly, Fall-only admissions now that the cycle is 4 terms long instead of 2. Only cs_core courses are offered optionally (same 'squeeze CS, leave everyone else ample' philosophy as section_demand_percentile above) -- math/english/gen_ed courses are deliberately excluded: they're tier-2 (lowest) enrollment priority, so cs_core crowds them out of every regular term's load cap, building a multi-year backlog that would swamp a 1-section bonus offering (observed: ~1000+ capacity blocks on a single gen-ed course vs ~250 on the CS gateway) -- a real, if disproportionate, finding worth knowing about even though it's out of scope for this illustrative default.
_sections_note: Per-term seats for a course = course_sections[code] x seats_per_section. CS courses (cs_core, cs_elective) are auto-calibrated by scripts/size_sections.py to the section_demand_percentile of per-term demand (0.75) so seats bind on CS gateways during enrolment bulges; non-CS courses are sized to full peak so they never bottleneck. Edit individual courses to add/cut sections. HAND-TUNE: CMPS303 (2->3) and CMPS350 (2->3) were raised one section above the demand-percentile baseline. Both were capacity-binding gateways (CMPS303 is the top capacity block; CMPS350 gates the CMPS493 compound rule), and relieving them lifts the 30-seed graduation rate from 0.708 to 0.716 toward QU's 0.723 benchmark. Lowering dropout_base_hazard does NOT move graduation (it only trades dropped for censored), so this gap is capacity-structural, not a dropout-parameter miss.
_registration_tier_note: Seat-priority bands by completed_ch, checked in descending order (registration_tier 0 = registers first). QU's real priority-registration policy; a different institution redefines its own bands here, not in student.py::registration_tier.
_enrollment_priority_note: Phase-1 desired-enrollment order, tried after retakes (which always come first and are not part of this list). Each tier is {categories: [...], min_ch?: N}; a student's eligible courses are bucketed into the first tier whose categories contains the course's category AND whose min_ch (default 0) the student's completed_ch already meets, then tiers are filled in order up to the load cap. A category absent from every tier, or gated by an unmet min_ch, is simply not enrolled that term. This is QU CS's policy (required courses, then cs_electives once junior-standing CH is reached, then service/gen-ed courses) expressed as data; a different program redefines its own tiers/categories here, not in student.py.
_dropout_note: Dropout has two causes. PRIMARY (GPA): each term a student with completed_ch >= probation_min_ch and gpa < dropout_gpa_floor leaves with probability base_hazard*(1+(floor-gpa)), doubled (early_multiplier) in their first dropout_early_sem_cutoff semesters. SECONDARY (gateway): failing one course dropout_fails_threshold times drops them with prob dropout_prob_on_repeated_fail. CALIBRATION SCOPE: the only QU anchor (scripts/analyze_qu_data.py) is the AGGREGATE 72.3% 12-semester graduation rate — the open data is semester-level counts, not student-level histories, so dropout TIMING cannot be validated. The early_multiplier front-loading is a face-validity assumption, not a data fit. Note base_hazard does not control the graduation rate (it trades dropped<->censored); graduation is bound by gateway capacity (see _sections_note).
